cluster.scope: Fresh install, onboarding progression, provider skip, identity setup, starter subscription, error recovery, relaunch persistence, and accessibility gates.
cluster.title: Foundation And First Run

common_capture_requirements: Capture at least one step screenshot, one semantic UI tree, one command/log reference, and one generated report-page viewport check for every scenario.
Run light appearance first; add dark appearance when the flow includes persistent navigation chrome, sheet chrome, or visual state that could invert poorly.
Verify 44 pt minimum tap targets, safe-area reachability, Dynamic Type behavior, VoiceOver labels, contrast, Reduce Motion, and Reduce Transparency before scoring UI/UX above 2.
Record NMP/RMP boundary observations: Rust-owned state/projections, thin native rendering, replayable clocks, and fail-closed provider or network behavior.
execution_principles: Do not mark a scenario executed from this manifest alone; attach simulator/device evidence first.
Every screenshot set must include source commit, app build, device/OS, appearance, Dynamic Type, Reduce Motion, Reduce Transparency, network mode, and redaction state.
Glass and material critique must focus on navigation or control chrome only; dense content surfaces need readable solid or semantic backgrounds.
Every actionable defect found during execution must be filed before a scenario can leave incomplete.

issue_filing_gates.file_issue_if: Any main-tab, library, player, account, or seeded app state is reachable before onboarding completion.
Cold launch shows a blank screen, spinner loop, clipped CTA, or inaccessible first action for more than the budget.

performance_metrics.method: XCTest launch metric or xcodebuild timestamp from process launch to first captured onboarding UI tree.
performance_metrics.name: Cold launch to first onboarding frame

scenarios.runbook: Erase simulator/app-group data, launch Pod0, and stop on the first onboarding page before tapping any primary action.

screenshot_requirements.description: First onboarding page immediately after cold launch.
Semantic UI tree proving no main-tab controls are reachable behind onboarding.

scenarios.title: Onboarding page 1 appears and no main-tab state is reachable behind it

ui_ux_liquid_glass_checks.check: Onboarding behaves as a true first-run gate with no navigable app state behind it.
Any glass-like treatment is limited to chrome or controls and preserves contrast over the launch backdrop.

issue_filing_gates.file_issue_if: A primary action skips, repeats, or reorders pages without an explicit user choice.
The primary completion path is clipped, disabled without recovery, or unreachable by touch or VoiceOver.

performance_metrics.method: Timestamp primary tap to next stable UI tree for every page transition.
performance_metrics.name: Onboarding page transition latency

scenarios.runbook: Start from a fresh install and advance through every onboarding page using only visible primary controls.

screenshot_requirements.description: One screenshot per onboarding page, named in visible order.
must_include: back or skip affordance when present

screenshot_requirements.description: Final app-entry screen after the last onboarding action.

scenarios.title: Page order, progress indicators, and final app entry are stable

ui_ux_liquid_glass_checks.check: Page order, progress, and back/skip behavior match the user's mental model and do not strand the user.
Headlines, body copy, and buttons remain readable without truncating critical meaning.

issue_filing_gates.file_issue_if: Skipping AI blocks library, playback, or first-run completion.
The skip path sends a live provider request, persists phantom credentials, or reports a connected provider.

performance_metrics.method: Inspect redacted logs or cassette registry for OpenRouter/Ollama/STT/TTS calls while skipping setup.
performance_metrics.name: Provider network calls during skip path

scenarios.runbook: Complete onboarding by choosing the visible AI/provider skip path, then open Library, playback, and Settings AI.

screenshot_requirements.description: AI setup page with skip or not-now path before selection.
Settings AI after onboarding completes with providers disconnected.

scenarios.title: Provider settings remain disconnected without blocking library or playback

ui_ux_liquid_glass_checks.check: Skip copy explains consequences without making AI setup feel mandatory for library or playback.
Disconnected provider state is visible, stable, and not confused with a failure state.

issue_filing_gates.file_issue_if: Settings identity does not match the name entered during onboarding.
Screenshots, UI tree, logs, or export state expose nsec/private key material.

performance_metrics.method: Timestamp completion tap to stable identity UI tree with the entered name.
performance_metrics.name: Identity projection settle after completion

scenarios.runbook: Enter a unique agent display name during onboarding, complete setup, and open Settings identity/account details.

screenshot_requirements.description: Onboarding name-entry page with typed test name before completion.
Settings identity screen showing the same display name from persisted kernel state.

scenarios.title: Settings identity shows the same display name from kernel state

ui_ux_liquid_glass_checks.check: Keyboard, field label, focus state, and primary action remain reachable and understandable.
The name appears consistently across onboarding completion and Settings identity without native-side remapping.

issue_filing_gates.file_issue_if: The subscribed show appears only after manual refresh, relaunch, or reseeding.
The cassette is missing/unmapped, subscription duplicates the show, or the row lacks title/artwork/metadata.

performance_metrics.method: Timestamp subscribe tap, cassette replay response, and first Library UI tree containing the show.
performance_metrics.name: Subscribe action to Library row visible

scenarios.runbook: Use the starter feed cassette, subscribe from onboarding suggestions, then open Library without manual refresh.

screenshot_requirements.description: Library after onboarding subscription completes with the show visible.

scenarios.title: The show appears in Library without manual refresh

ui_ux_liquid_glass_checks.check: Subscription progress and success feedback are visible and do not require hidden refresh knowledge.
The new Library row gives the show identity priority over decorative chrome.

issue_filing_gates.file_issue_if: Invalid RSS submit crashes, loops, hangs, or traps the user with no recovery.
The error is generic, offscreen, inaccessible, or does not explain the next recoverable action.

performance_metrics.method: Inspect structured logs or cassette miss logs around the invalid submit.
performance_metrics.name: Network or parser retry loop count

scenarios.runbook: Enter an invalid RSS URL during onboarding, submit it once, inspect the inline error, then recover by continuing or correcting input.

screenshot_requirements.description: Inline invalid-RSS error immediately after one submit.
must_include: invalid URL value or redacted placeholder

screenshot_requirements.description: Recovered state after correcting, clearing, or skipping the invalid feed.

scenarios.title: An inline recoverable error appears and onboarding can continue

ui_ux_liquid_glass_checks.check: Error copy is specific, placed near the field, and leaves a clear recovery path.
VoiceOver and focus order announce the invalid state without trapping the user.

issue_filing_gates.file_issue_if: Onboarding reappears after completion and preserved-data relaunch.
Relaunch creates duplicate identity/account/library state or loses completed setup state.

performance_metrics.method: Timestamp process launch to stable main-shell UI tree after force quit.
performance_metrics.name: Preserved-data cold launch to main shell

scenarios.runbook: Complete onboarding, force quit, relaunch with preserved app data, and verify the main shell appears instead of onboarding.

screenshot_requirements.description: Completed onboarding state immediately before force quit.

scenarios.title: Onboarding does not reappear

ui_ux_liquid_glass_checks.check: The user returns to app state with no duplicate setup burden or contradictory welcome copy.
Restored main navigation chrome remains readable and does not ghost onboarding content behind glass.

issue_filing_gates.file_issue_if: Interrupted relaunch duplicates identity/account state, jumps to main shell, or resumes with contradictory step state.
The app restarts onboarding without explaining lost partial progress or providing a stable recovery path.

performance_metrics.method: Timestamp first frame to stable restored step or safe restart route.
performance_metrics.name: Interrupted onboarding restore decision

scenarios.runbook: Advance halfway through onboarding, force quit before completion, relaunch, and record whether the app restores the step or restarts safely.

screenshot_requirements.description: Partially completed onboarding step before force quit.

must_include: restored step or explicit safe restart

scenarios.title: It restores the correct step or restarts safely without duplicate identity creation

ui_ux_liquid_glass_checks.check: Restoration is understandable and avoids asking the user to redo irreversible identity work.
The current step and next action are visually obvious after relaunch.

search_command: npx skills search "iOS mobile UI UX liquid glass native polish validation"
title: Foundation Onboarding Screenshot Wave 001
